course_details: Key Tips: Skip to main content Mailing Address Address: IIT Bombay, Powai, Mumbai 400 076, Maharashtra, India Phone: +91 (22) 2572 2545 Working Hours 9:15 am to 5:45 pm (Monday to Friday) How to reach IIT Bombay Here's how you can reach IIT Bombay (located in Powai, Mumbai) using various modes of transport: By Train Central Line: Closest Station: Kanjurmarg or Vikhroli. From Kanjurmarg (approximately 3 km from IIT Bombay): You can take an auto-rickshaw or a taxi to the campus. From Vikhroli: Similarly, auto-rickshaws or taxis are readily available to reach the campus. Western Line: Closest Station: Andheri. From Andheri (approximately 10 km from IIT Bombay): You can take an auto-rickshaw, taxi, or a BEST bus heading towards Powai. By Metro Closest Metro Station: Saki Naka (Mumbai Metro Line 1/ Blue line), Aarey JVLR (Mumbai metro Aqua line) From Saki Naka (approximately 4 km from IIT Bombay): You can take an auto-rickshaw to reach the campus. From Aarey JVLR: You can take an auto-rickshaw to reach the campus. By Bus BEST buses frequently connect IIT Bombay to different parts of Mumbai. Popular routes include: From Kanjurmarg or Vikhroli stations: Look for buses heading to Hiranandani Gardens or IIT Main Gate. From Sion, Dadar, or other areas: Direct buses to IIT Bombay or Powai are available. Bus stops near IIT Bombay include IIT Main Gate, IIT Market Gate, and Hiranandani Gardens. You can download and use the mIndicator app to get information on bus numbers and bus routes. By Airport From Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj International Airport/ Domestic Airport: Options: Prepaid Taxi or Ride-Sharing Apps: You can take a pre-paid taxi or book an Uber or Ola at the airport. Auto-Rickshaw: You can also take a pre-paid auto at the airport. Metro: Take the metro from Airport Road station to Saki Naka station, then an auto-rickshaw to IIT Bombay. Chalo bus: You can download the Chalo bus app and enter IIT Bombay or Powai IIT Main Gate or IIT Market Gate as your destination. You can check the available bus numbers and routes. By Road IIT Bombay is accessible via the Eastern Express Highway and Jogeshwari-Vikhroli Link Road (JVLR). Private vehicles, auto-rickshaws, or taxis can bring you directly to the campus. Key Tips: Always confirm with the driver (especially auto-rickshaws) that they are taking you to "IIT Bombay, Powai," as other campuses have similar names. Traffic in Mumbai can be heavy during peak hours, so plan your trip accordingly. course_details: Indian Institute of Technology Madras Fee Structure: fees=Rs. 2500 | IITM Integrated Fee Payment Portal Skip to main content Home Academics Academic Services Fees Fee Structure Credits Gaurav Arora M Tech, Computer Science Engg Semester Fees Students/scholars are required to pay their Semester fees as per the category they belong to. The fee structures are different for Indian and foreign nationals. Image Credits Chandra Kishore PhD Biotechnology All students / scholars (including HTTA/HTRA students /scholars) have to pay their semester fees directly online as per fee structure applicable to the category to which they belong. Dual degree students have to pay their M.Tech fees from 9th semester onwards. Students / scholars are not permitted to pay the Institute fees in installments; the full fees should be paid on or before the deadline otherwise failure to do so will result in a penalty of Rs. 2500 for late payment. IITM Integrated Fee Payment Portal To pay the Institute Fees and Hostel Fees - visit https://fees.iitm.ac.in/. . Day Scholars must pay applicable fees as prescribed in the Hostel Fee Circular. This portal will enable payment of Institute fees (including outstanding), Hostel Fees (including outstanding), Advance Dining Charges and Optional fees from CCW by Students of IIT Madras who fall under the categories Current, Not enrolled and RKA. Note: : If the transaction fails and money is deducted from your account, then please wait 3 to 5 working days for money to be credited back to your account. If the money is not credited automatically, then contact Finance & Accounts Section at chequeunit@iitm.ac.in with your Roll number, Name and Transaction ID. The following salient features will be available in the Integrated Fee Payment Portal. This portal allows payment of fees by student or by their parents/guardian or others. Passcode will be sent to all students on their Institute smail id. Students are requested not to share this code to anyone except to those who will be paying their fees. For New Joinees, the passcode will be sent to the registered email id provided during admission process. Students are advised to generate a fresh passcode once they receive their Institute email account (smail account). This portal enables payment of Institute Fees (including outstanding), Hostel Fees (including outstanding), and Advance Dining charges. Optional fees for events like Shaastra, Saarang and E-summit can also be paid through this portal. The aforementioned fees have to be paid only through this portal. This portal facilitates submission of loan details by students regarding the loan amount that has been credited into IIT Madras bank account for easy reconciliation (both for Institute and Hostel fees). This portal enables availing refund/ adjustment in the next semester, if excess credit is available. This portal is optimized for Desktop and Laptop view only. For Students availing bank loan for paying Institute Fees, please use the bank account details given in the link, https://fees.iitm.ac.in/assets/circular/tution_fee_loan.pdf For Students availing bank loan for paying Hostel Fees, please contact the Office of the Chairman, Council of Wardens (CCW Office) for Bank details. course_details: IIT Kanpur Sets Record with 672 Job Offers on Day 1 of 2025–26 Placement Season Set your preference Reset All Font Scaling Reset - Default + Page Scaling Reset - Default + Color Adjustment Reset Greyscale High Saturation Low Saturation High Contrast Light Contrast High Brightness Light Brightness Quick Links Admission Announcements Departments Office Automation Pingala Webmail News Researches Home Academics Research Incubation People Administration Services Connect Home Press Releases IIT Kanpur Sets Record with 672 Job Offers on Day 1 of 2025–26 Placement Season IIT Kanpur Sets Record with 672 Job Offers on Day 1 of 2025–26 Placement Season IIT Kanpur Sets Record with 672 Job Offers on Day 1 of 2025–26 Placement Season Kanpur , 3 December 2025 Source : Information and Media Outreach Cell, IIT Kanpur Kanpur, 03 December 2025 : The Indian Institute of Technology Kanpur kicked off its 2025–26 campus placement season with a record-breaking performance on 1st December, receiving 672 job offers on Day 1, the highest Day 1 tally to date. This represents a remarkable 16% increase over last year’s Day-1 placements, highlighting IIT Kanpur’s growing industry appeal and the strong demand for its graduates. With some results still pending, the count is expected to rise further. By the end of Day 1, 627 students had secured job offers, including Pre-Placement Offers (PPOs), reflecting a significant 20% rise in placements compared to the previous season. PPOs played a major role in this achievement, with 253 offers recorded, marking a strong 27% increase over last year. On the international front, nine students secured overseas offers, demonstrating IIT Kanpur’s global relevance. Top global and Indian recruiters actively engaged with students across disciplines, reaffirming IIT Kanpur’s reputation as a premier talent hub. Over 250 companies have shown interest in hiring IITK students this year. Leading recruiters include Accenture, BlackRock, HSBC, SAP, Airbus, PwC, Navi, Qualcomm, Deutsche Bank, and many more. The Students’ Placement Office and institute leadership congratulated all the selected students, expressed appreciation to the recruiting partners, and emphasised that this record-breaking Day 1 reflects the students’ dedication, the faculty’s mentorship, and the continued trust of the industry in IIT Kanpur. With several more companies scheduled to visit and many students yet to be placed, the 2025–26 placement season promises even more exciting career opportunities in the weeks ahead. ABOUT IIT KANPUR The Indian Institute of Technology Kanpur, established in 1959 by an Act of Parliament, holds the distinction of being recognized as an Institute of National Importance by the Government of India. Renowned for its excellence in science and engineering education, IIT Kanpur has made significant contributions to research and development over the decades. Its expansive, lush green campus spans 1,055 acres and hosts a rich array of academic and research resources. The institute comprises 20 departments, 27 centres, three interdisciplinary programs, and three specialized schools across engineering, science, design, humanities, and management disciplines. With over 570 full-time faculty members and more than 9,500 students, IIT Kanpur remains a leader in fostering innovation and academic rigour. Joint Entrance Examination (Main) The Ministry of Education [erstwhile Ministry of Human Resources Development (MHRD)], Government of India (GOI) has established National Testing Agency (NTA) as an independent autonomous and self-sustained premier testing organization under Society Registration Act 1860 for conducting efficient, transparent and international standards tests in order to assess the competency of candidates for admissions to premier higher education institutions. The Department of Higher Education, Ministry of Human Resource Development, Government of India has entrusted the responsibility of conducting Joint Entrance Examination JEE (Main) to the NTA from 2019 onwards. Admission criteria to Undergraduate Engineering Programs at NITs, IIITs, other Centrally Funded Technical Institutions (CFTI), Institutions funded by participating State Governments, and other Institutions shall include the performance in the class 12/equivalent qualifying Examination and in the Joint Entrance Examination, JEE (Main). The (B. E. /B. Tech.) of JEE (Main) will also be an eligibility test for the JEE (Advanced), which the candidate has to take if he/she is aspiring for admission to the undergraduate programs offered by the Indian Institute of Technology (IITs). Joint Seat Allocation Authority (JoSAA) The Joint Seat Allocation Authority (JoSAA) has been set up by the Ministry of Education [erstwhile Ministry of Human Resources Development (MHRD)] to manage and regulate the joint seat allocation to 114 institutes for the academic year 2021-22. This includes 23 IITs, 31 NITs, IIEST Shibpur, 26 IIITs, and 33 Other technical institutes funded fully or partially by central or state governments (other Government Funded Technical Institutes or other-GFTIS). Admission to all the academic programs offered by these Institutes will be made through a single platform. Qualifying Examination Admitting Institutes JEE (Advanced) IITs JEE (Main) / B.E./ B.Tech. Central Seat Allocation Board (CSAB) The Ministry of Human Resource Development, Government of India took a policy decision in 2002 to conduct an All India Engineering Entrance Examination (AIEEE) annually, doing away with many of the large number of entrance examinations that were being conducted till then by various institutes in the country for admission to undergraduate degree programmes in the disciplines of Engineering/Technology and Architecture/Planning. (.). These are level wise equivalent degrees. They are considered to be integrated degrees for two reasons: (i) the degree program includes several cross disciplinary common courses such as basic math and science and, (ii) no intermediate degree, like, B.Sc., B.Sc. (Hons.), B.A., B.A. (Hons), etc. is awarded. These degrees are modular in structure and academic requirements are spelt out in terms of the number of courses studied rather than the number of years. All our programmes are designed in a way to facilitate completion within eight semesters. course_details: Made by Dr. Ayush Yadav Made by Dr. Ayush Yadav: rankings=mation: June 20 | KGMU Assistant Information for: Faculty & Staff Patients Alumni A+ A- On Screen Reader Placement Cell Year of establishment or formation: June 20, 2022 Brief History The Placement Cell was established in 2022 with the vision of bridging the gap between the medical education and clinical research. It serves as a dedicated unit to support student career advancement by facilitating clinical placements, research opportunities, and collaborations with healthcare institutions. The cell aligns with KGMU's mission of academic excellence and service by fostering practical exposure, professional skill development, and structured career guidance for its students. Vision, Mission, and Objectives of the Placement Cell Vision: To connect students with clinical, academic, and research opportunities, shaping them into skilled healthcare professionals. Mission: To prepare students for medical careers through hands-on exposure, academic collaborations, and professional development. Objectives: To enable placements, internships, and training while offering career guidance and skill enhancement support. course_details: IIM Ahmedabad MBA Programme Academics: rankings=CAT-2025 | mation Bulletin PGP 2026 | MBA Admissions: rankings=CAT-2025 | mation Bulletin PGP 2026 | Important Links Home Menu Academics Skip to main content Admissions MBA Admissions Overview The two-year full-time Post Graduate Programme in Management (PGP) is the flagship MBA programme of IIMA. It is a residential programme for graduates from different disciplines wishing to choose a career in management. Its main objective is to develop competent professional managers. The admission process consists of the nationally administered Common Admission Test (CAT) for domestic applicants, and GMAT for overseas Indian applicants or foreign nationals, followed by analytical writing test and personal interviews for shortlisted candidates. The students are selected on the basis of the applicable (CAT/GMAT) exam scores, past academic performance and performance in the analytical writing test and personal interviews. Selected students have scholastic achievements in disciplines such as arts, commerce, science and professional streams such as engineering, technology, and medicine. Many of them also have achievements in extra-curricular and organizational activities. Some of the qualities that characterize past students are high levels of initiative and energy, capacity for hard work, strong task orientation, willingness to learn, and a temperament suitable for teamwork. The PGP classes have a mix of fresh graduates and persons with work experience. course_details: Career Development and Placement Skip to main content About Administration Announcements Academics Executive Education Faculty & Research Alumni Career Development and Placement Library Joka Culture Sustainability Institute Gift Shop Director's Message Board of Governors Deans Campus Life International Office Contacts Tenders Media Room Jobs@IIMC Announcements Admissions All Programmes Doctoral (Ph.D.) Executive Ph.D. MBA MBAEX PGPEX-VLM PGDBA CEMS MIM About Executive Education Short Duration Programmes Calendar Long Duration Programmes Calendar Asynchronous Programmes Connect With Us ACE 2025 Faculty & Research Home Faculty Directory Retired Faculty Directory Academic Groups Centers of Excellence Publications Conferences & Seminars Faculty Portal Recruitments Professor of Practice Campus Infrastructure Life @ IIMC Student Council MBAEx Placements PGPEX VLM Placements PGDBA Placements Contact Us Overview Alumni Magazine Distinguished Alumnus Award Alumni News Alumni Chapters Alumni Portal Give Arijit Mukherji Memorial Lecture Series Foundation Day Board of Governors The Director Deans Programme Chairpersons Coordinators of the Centers of Excellences Career Development and Placement Career Development and Placement Prof. Sudarshan Kumar Chairperson, Career Development and Placement Committee Career Development and Placement Office In a world where competition grows by the minute and multiplies by the hour, placements have become a yardstick for excellence. IIM Calcutta is widely considered the best in the country in this respect. That is testimony to the quality of its students and the strength of our corporate relationships that have been built over the years. Students of the institute enjoy the varied kaleidoscope and are exposed to a variety of functional areas and sectors. Job locations encompass the globe, from New York and London to Shanghai and Hong Kong and even the Middle East. And the list just goes on increasing every year. Retail majors, private equity firms, investment holding companies, and leading investment banks all come down to Joka to pick up the brightest minds in India every year. Our Placement Team at IIM Calcutta is crucial in creating various engagements with corporates, public sector undertakings, and international business firms. Throughout the year, the team actively engages with recruiters to ensure successful Summer Internships and Final placement opportunities for our students. We have always believed in building everlasting bonds with our corporate partners in our desire to be the best. Excellence they say flows beyond boundaries and we at IIM Calcutta believe the journey has just begun as we strive to scale new heights and explore uncharted terrains. The recent years have witnessed several new companies on campus, once again proving the age-old adage “Whatever you do, wherever you go, getting better never stops". course_details: Indian Institute of Management Kozhikode - IIMK Summer Placements: fees=rs | Skip to Main Content - A + Summer Placements Home Placements PGP/PGP-FIN/PGP-LSM Summer Placements Placements PGP/PGP-FIN/PGP-LSM Summer Placements Final Placements Chairpersons Message Placements Calendar Recruiters Guide Contact Us PGP-BL Recruiters guide Placement Report Summer Placements Report Summer Placements Report 2024 Summer Placements Report - Archives Summer Placements Report 2023 Summer Placements Report 2022 Summer Placements Report 2021 Summer Placements Report 2020 Summer Placements Report 2019 Summer Placements Report 2018 Summer Placements Report 2017 Summer Placements Report 2016 Summer Placements Report 2015 Summer Placements Report 2014 Summer Placements Report 2013 Summer Placements Report 2012 Summer Internship - The Concept Summer Internship is an integral part of the PGP course at Indian Institute of Management Kozhikode. During their first year, students are trained in functional areas of Marketing, Finance, Consulting, Economics, Organizational Behaviour and Human Resource Management, Operations and Information Technology which orient them towards a business culture. The courses aim at molding their work ethic and developing skills highly valued by the contemporary industry. At the completion of the first year in PGP course, students spend 8-10 weeks during the summer in the industry. Students are exposed to a real time business environment, and take up responsibilities and goals which aim at being mutually beneficial for both the student and the organization. It serves as a platform for applying the management theories learnt in the classroom and helps in providing clarity about the functional area that they desire to specialize in. Internships thus have a crucial influence on the career path and choice of organization by a student. Why Should Companies Recruit Interns? Internships have a distinct edge over interview-based final recruitment as a method of evaluating potential employees. Deserving candidates often get Pre-Placement Offers, and students at IIM Kozhikode have enjoyed a particularly special relationship with their recruiters in this regard. Summer interns offer a fresh perspective to projects. They are in a unique position to provide an objective and unbiased point of view about the organization’s work processes. The students also act as brand ambassadors for the organization on campus, acting as spokespersons and spreading awareness about the company in general. Recruitment Process The recruitment process for summer internship commences from the first week of October. Companies can plan interaction with the students, either on any prior day or on the day they visit the campus for summer recruitment. Companies can choose any method suitable to them for selecting summer interns. Companies are encouraged to send us their Job Description at least 15 days in advance. This will enable us to forward the resumes of interested candidates, and any other information that the organizations may require, before their visit to the campus. Since students are obliged to accept the first summer job offered to them, companies are requested to select students well in advance. Indian Institute of Management Kozhikode IIMK Campus P. course_details: Faculty of Management Studies Student Life Contact Us Important Links Alumni Portal About About the Institute Dean's Welcome Facilities NAAC SSR Report Alumni Community Diversity And Inclusion Programmes MBA Full Time MBA-Executive (Evening Programme) MBA-Executive HCA (Evening Programme) Doctoral Programme General Management Programme Management Development Faculty & Research Our Faculty Directory Research And Papers Conferences Student Life Student Life Management Science Association Student Committees Exchange Program Grievance Redressal Admissions Online Admissions Portal Results & Notifications Online Fee Deposit Corporate Relations Recruiters@FMS Corporate Competitions Placement Reports Build a Relationship with Us Batch Profiles Contact Corporate Relations and Placement Team Student Life Student Life Student life at FMS is vibrant and multifaceted, offering an experience that extends well beyond the classroom. With a wide array of activities, students are encouraged to engage in academic, cultural, and social events that foster personal and professional growth. From participating in industry workshops and guest lectures to joining student societies and organizing national-level fests, our students are immersed in a dynamic environment. These experiences not only enhance their learning but also build strong networks and leadership skills. The campus atmosphere is one of collaboration, innovation, and continuous development , making FMS a place where students thrive both academically and personally. The balance between rigorous academics and a rich extracurricular life ensures that students at FMS are well-prepared for their future careers, equipped with a holistic understanding of the business world and the skills needed to succeed. Management Science Association At FMS, the two-year MBA journey extends beyond traditional classroom learning, involving students in a diverse range of activities orchestrated by the Management Science Association (MSA). MSA's mission is to promote holistic development by blending academic excellence with practical experience. Through participation in various initiatives, including academic competitions, industry workshops, community projects, and campus events, students gain invaluable insights and skills. MSA’s comprehensive approach, from onboarding new students to managing placements, ensures a well-rounded and enriching experience. The MSA team is committed to enhancing campus life, creating an environment that supports both personal and professional growth. course_details: CAMPUS PLACEMENT CELL CAMPUS PLACEMENT CELL Home CAMPUS PLACEMENT CELL Recruitment Brochure 2025-26 CAMPUS PLACEMENT CELL The Placement Cell at St. Stephen’s College is a student-run body that has, since its inception, always strived to match the repute and merit that the College is distinguished for. The student body is a think tank of competent and qualified individuals capable of achieving excellence in every field. The Placement Cell has seen exceptional success in the last academic year. The academic year of 2025-26 showed extremely promising statistics with an average base pay of INR 10.5 Lacs per annum. World’s top consultancy firms – McKinsey & Company, Boston Consulting Group, Kearney, EY Parthenon, LEK Consulting and Praxis Global Alliance to name a few, along with companies associated with the banking and financial sector- Morgan Stanley, JP Morgan, Nomura, American Express, Citi and D.E Shaw, among others, have been our past recruiters. The highest base pay package extended was 24.5 Lacs per annum. St. Stephen’s College also bears the distinction of being the first undergraduate institution in the country to have recruitment from firms such as The Lehman Brothers and Merrill Lynch, London. St. Stephen’s College was the first college in Delhi University to host an Internship Fair with the aim of providing students and companies a platform for mutual growth. The Internship Conclave is held with the purpose of inviting firms to hire summer interns. Companies like Deutsche Bank, Hindustan Unilever Limited, Standard Chartered, Nomura, JSW, CitiBank, Ernst & Young and E&H Foundation are some companies who hired Summer Interns from St. Stephen’s College in the past. For information on the Campus Placement Cells recruitment policy and structure, please go through the Recruitment Brochure 2025-26. Suman Bhanoo Please contact for any query related to admission 2020-21 Email: admission2020@lsr.edu.in Phone: 8929410191 Candidates applying for admission to LSR College have to use the following email ids of respective departments to send their fresh or clear documents. Department Email id Commerce commercedoc2020@lsr.edu.in Economics economicsdoc2020@lsr.edu.in English englishdoc2020@lsr.edu.in Hindi hindidoc2020@lsr.edu.in History historydoc2020@lsr.edu.in Mathematics mathsdoc2020@lsr.edu.in Statistics statisticsdoc2020@lsr.edu.in Philosophy philosophydoc2020@lsr.edu.in Political Sciences polsciencedoc2020@lsr.edu.in Psychology psychologydoc2020@lsr.edu.in Sanskrit sanskritdoc2020@lsr.edu.in Sociology sociologydoc2020@lsr.edu.in Journalism journalismdoc2020@lsr.edu.in BA Programme baprogrammedoc2020@lsr.edu.in Elementary Education beleddoc2020@lsr.edu.in Question: Can I submit my parents certificate now and submit mine later? Answer: For Admissions 2023-24 you need to upload certificate (SC, ST, OBC-NCL, PwBD, KM, EWS) issued in the name of the Candidate only. Question: Can I get admission at LSR directly? Answer: You must have appeared for CUET and then apply for LSR (College + Program preference) as you fill the CSAS form for University of Delhi. Question: What are the Course taught at LSR? Answer: Please visit the college website www.lsr.edu.in for information about various courses taught at LSR or https://lsr.edu.in/admissions/courses-offered/ Question: I have studied Business Mathematics in class XII. How can I apply for B.Sc. (Hons.) Mathematics program? Answer: You must appear in Mathematics at CUET (UG) – 2023. Question: I did not appear in the CUET (UG) – 2023. Will my Class XII marks be considered for admissions this year? Answer: For admission to University of Delhi, it is mandatory for the candidate to appear in CUET (UG) – 2023 in those subjects in which s(he) is appearing/ has passed Class XII. Question: I appeared in CUET (UG) – 2022 but did not appear in CUET (UG) – 2023. Will my CUET scores of last year be considered for admission in this academic year? Answer: Only the scores obtained in CUET (UG) – 2023 will be considered for admissions to University of Delhi in the academic year 2023-24. Question: I have applied for CUET (UG) – 2023 and during the registration I have added a few undergraduate programs at the University of Delhi, but there was no provision to select Colleges. How will I know where my admission will be? Answer: After the successful registration of CUET (UG) – 2023, you will be required to fill the CSAS (UG) – 23. In CSAS form of the University of Delhi, you should fill the preferences for the Programs and Colleges in which you wish to study at the University of Delhi. Keep looking at the admission website on a regular basis. Question: Can I apply for B.A. (Hons) Hindi at the University of Delhi if I have not studied and passed Hindi in the Class XII board examination? Answer: For admission to B.A. (Hons) Hindi the applicant must have studied and passed Hindi at the Class XII board examination and should have also appeared in Hindi in CUET (UG) – 2023. Question: While applying to CUET (UG) – 2023 do I have to upload Class XII marks of all the subjects or only for the Best five/four? Answer: You should upload marks for all the subjects you have appeared in Class XII in case your result is declared. Question: How will I apply for the Extra-Curricular Activities quota at the University of Delhi? Answer: Appear in CUET (UG) – 2023 and wait for the subsequent announcements of the University of Delhi in this regard. Question: Is there any weightage to Class XII board marks for taking admission in undergraduate programs at the University of Delhi? Answer: Class XII marks will be considered for tie breaking, if any, during the seat allocation through CSAS. Question: How will colleges at the University of Delhi be allotted after the declaration of CUET (UG) – 2023 results? Answer: After the registration process of CUET (UG) – 2023, you will have to fill the Common Seat Allocation System -Undergraduate 2023 (CSAS(UG)) – 23 form of the University of Delhi. Details of selecting the Program/s and College/s will be published on the website. Keep looking at the admission website for all updated information. Question: For admission to B.Com. course_details: Skip to content Alumna Cell Since its inception in 1956, Lady Shri Ram College for women has produced a long line of distinguished alumni. Eminent alumni are found in any Indian roll call of politics, literature, industry, entertainment, sports and more. The Alumna cell is a student body with the aim to foster and bridge the interaction between the institute and its Alumni. We, in the Alumni Cell, seek to reach out, maintain and strengthen close ties with our Alumni. The Lady Shri Ram College Alumni Association is called ELSA. For more information on ELSA please check out https://lsralumna.org/ . The Placement Cell The Placement Cell at LSR is committed to guiding students as they navigate their career journeys. The Cell works to connect students with leading recruiters across industries through campus placements and internship programs. Beyond recruitment, we focus on holistic development—organizing skill-building workshops, resume reviews, mock interviews, and mentorship sessions to help students grow professionally. With a student-centric approach and strong industry partnerships, the Cell strives to empower every student and bring in excellent opportunities for them. The Placement Cell is headed by Coordinators, Sub-Coordinators, a Research & Training Head, a Public Relations Head, and a Treasurer, who are in turn supported by an efficient team of internship and placement Department Representatives, a dedicated team of members, and core team members who assist the coordinators in logistical arrangements. Staff Advisors Mr. T. Kannan, Convener, Placement Cell – hankan07@gmail.com Dr. Renu Kaul, Co-Convener, Placement Cell – renukaul62@gmail.com Dr. Shama Norein Major – shamanorienmajor@lsr.du.ac.in Dr. Shrawan Kumar Pandey – shrawankumarpandey@lsr.du.ac.in Dr. Siksha Deepak – siksha.siksha@gmail.com Ms. Akanksha Sethi – aakankshasethi@lsr.du.ac.in Ms. Vimanshi – vimanshisolanki@lsr.du.ac.in Mr. Rajeev – rajeev.yadav8@gmail.com Senior Union Senior Coordinators- Brinda Thamman . Dhiya Shine , Sonal Kedia , Sheen Madan , Arpita Grover , Vanshika Jain Research and Training Heads- Mansi Sharma , Ritika Singh Diversity and Inclusion Coordinator- Mahi Gupta Guidance and Opportunities Head- Rakshita Maheshwari Junior Union Junior Coordinators- Vidhi Baishkyar , Rashi Agarwal , Anshika Sharma , Hritika Shah PR and Engagement Head- Avni Jindal Ideation and Training Head- Vibhuti Treasurer and Logistics Head- Sayesha Activities in 2025-26 The Placement Cell of LSR continued to uphold its legacy of excellence during 2025–26, facilitating a dynamic and successful recruitment cycle. The Cell witnessed participation from a diverse pool of over 50 organisations, offering students opportunities across consulting, finance, marketing, analytics, and product domains. Prominent recruiters included McKinsey & Company, Boston Consulting Group, Accenture Strategy & Consulting, Bain Capability Network, Meesho, BlackRock, ZS Associates, Amazon, and L.E.K. Consulting, among others. The Cell also welcomed new recruiters, further expanding the breadth of opportunities available to students. A total of 160+ offers were made during the placement season. The highest package offered stood at ₹36.25 LPA, while the average and median packages were ₹13.54 LPA and ₹13.1 LPA, respectively. The recruitment cycle saw strong representation in consulting roles, alongside significant opportunities in finance, data analytics, marketing, and product management. The internship cycle witnessed strong engagement, with more than 60 structured internships offered by over 12 organisations. Leading recruiters included L.E.K. Consulting, World Wide Technology, and McKinsey & Company. The highest stipend stood at ₹3,60,000 for an 8-week engagement, while the average stipend was ₹67,745, reflecting the quality and competitiveness of opportunities secured by students. The Placement Cell undertook key initiatives aimed at enhancing accessibility and preparedness. The Cell initiated its first-ever workshop series for Persons with Disabilities, conducting 4 sessions with distinguished speakers to foster inclusive career development and targeted guidance. The LSR Consulting Casebook 2.0 served as a comprehensive resource for students preparing for case interviews, compiling curated case studies, guesstimates, and interview experiences. The Humanities Career Handbook was introduced as a structured guide to document real and replicable placement pathways for students from humanities and social science backgrounds. By focusing on real recruitment journeys and profile-building strategies, the handbook sought to bridge the gap between academic training and industry expectations, enabling students to better navigate diverse career opportunities. The Cell organized over 25 skill-building sessions throughout the academic year, featuring distinguished speakers from leading organisations. course_details: Our Campuses Centre for Placement and Career Guidance Sidebar × Overview The Centre for Placements and Career Guidance ( CPCG ) serves as a vital bridge between our esteemed students and the ever-evolving realm of professional opportunities. By spearheading comprehensive career exploration initiatives, the CPCG empowers students to delve into diverse employment landscapes and discover fulfilling career paths aligned with their unique aspirations. Through personalized counseling sessions, dedicated advisors guide students in a journey of self-discovery, students delve deep, uncovering their inherent interests, aptitudes, and strengths, forming the bedrock for informed career choices. This individualized approach ensures each student embarks on a career journey crafted specifically for their potential. The Centre for Placements and Career Guidance ( CPCG ) serves as a vital bridge between our esteemed students and the ever-evolving realm of professional opportunities. By spearheading comprehensive career exploration initiatives, the CPCG empowers students to delve into diverse employment landscapes and discover fulfilling career paths aligned with their unique aspirations. Through personalized counseling sessions, dedicated advisors guide students in a journey of self-discovery, students delve deep, uncovering their inherent interests, aptitudes, and strengths, forming the bedrock for informed career choices. This individualized approach ensures each student embarks on a career journey crafted specifically for their potential. Furthermore, the CPCG champions the professional development of its students, equipping them with essential employability enhancement skills. Intensive training programs hone students' Resume writing, Interview techniques, and Group discussion strategies, instilling confidence and poise in their interactions with potential employers. A rigorous focus on logical reasoning and aptitude skills sharpens students' critical thinking and problem-solving abilities, ensuring they stand out in competitive job markets. This unwavering commitment to comprehensive preparation yields impressive results, with our students securing coveted positions in their desired fields and gaining admission to prestigious postgraduate and professional schools. Additionally, the university boasts state-of-the-art facilities that facilitate seamless campus recruitment drives, transforming the job search into a rewarding and enriching experience. In essence, CPCG serves as a powerful catalyst for student success. We unlock a world of possibilities, empowering our students to confidently navigate the professional landscape and realize their career aspirations. Functions Functions Identifying and facilitating professional training needs Leadership training for student representatives Networking with companies for Campus recruitment and training Preparing students for Campus Recruitment processes Facilitating smooth conduct of Campus Recruitment processes Processing feedback from Recruiters and informing Academic departments regarding gaps in employability skills Documenting post-placement feedback from students Sustaining Academia-Corporate interface for skilling, upskilling and reskilling Engaging Alumni for Career Guidance and Counselling Honors and Accreditation Placement Highlights Report : Top Recruiters Vision Nurturing Employable Graduates Mission To ensure every student is provided an opportunity for placement and to empower students with the knowledge skills and resources necessary to navigate their career path successfully Mission Objectives To create awareness among students about opportunities available as per their programme and competencies. To share and build required competencies among the students as per inputs from industry through respective departments. To identify and invite potential recruiters to the University for placements and to maintain and sustain relationship with the present and potential recruiters. To develop annual placement schedule and to be a single point of contact for the recruiters, academic departments and students for effective placement process. Build employability through skill development workshops. course_details: Our Campuses Sidebar Online Payment Portal Online Payment Instructions Detailed instructions are given in the login page of the web payment as to the procedure to be followed in case of completed transaction and payment confirmation slip received and also failed transaction. Pls follow the instruction carefully. Once “pay” - option is selected you will be directed for payment through net banking / credit card / debit card. You can choose the desired payment option and proceed. Once the payment is successful, you will get a payment confirmation slip and the payer has to keep the same for reference. In case the payment is not successful due to any reason you will get a display on the status of failure in payment. In case none of the above two happens, and there is heavy delay in any response from the system - if you have not proceeded with payment and not given card detail particulars, you may proceed from the beginning again and start the payment process again. In case you have given all the credit card / debit card details or net baking authorization for payment, and have not got any response, please check with your bankers or credit card company and see if your account is debited. If your bank account/cc is debited, please dont make any attempt to pay again. However, if your account is not debited in the bank, you have to make the payment and get payment successful confirmation. In any case, make a note of reference/transaction details in case of net banking or card payment. Privacy Policy The details provided by you shall be utilized only for the purpose of receiving the payments to be made by you to the institution. All data shall be kept secure, and shall not be divulged to anyone or utilized for any other purpose. Cancellation / Refund Policy There is no cancellation option available to end users once payment has been made. Additionally, refunds are not possible in such cases. In case of duplicate payment, payer/parent/student, kindly approach admin or finance or accounts department of the institution for refund with proof of the transaction reference/ your bank statement. Refund will be processed within 10-15 working days, respective payment gateway will send back to the issuing bank [user’s card banker] in batches for processing, which should approximately take 8-15 working days, depending on issuing banks policies. Important: by submitting a payment through the online-payments site you are agreeing to these terms and conditions including any updated changes in terms and conditions from time to time through our website. Disclaimer The articles, information and documents provided on this website are purely for information purpose and no legal commitment whatsoever are attached to the same in case of any inadvertent error that might have occurred due to unavoidable circumstances in spite of all the efforts put by the website management team. Register to participate in JNU's campus recruitment process Register Now Placement Cell Jawaharlal Nehru University Downloads Placement Cell Brochure Placement Policy Academic Calendar Notice Board Read More Greetings from JNU Placement Cell The JNU Placement Cell extends a warm welcome to all recruiting partners. As one of India's premier universities, Jawaharlal Nehru University is home to a diverse and accomplished student body nurtured by globally recognized faculty and a progressive academic environment. We take pride in facilitating internship and placement opportunities between recruiters and students across disciplines. Our students bring with them not only academic excellence but also a strong sense of social responsibility, leadership, and critical thinking—qualities shaped by JNU's unique educational ethos. The Placement Cell serves as a professional bridge between top-tier talent and leading organizations, fostering partnerships built on merit, integrity, and long-term value. We look forward to collaborating with you in shaping future leaders and building professional excellence. Accredited A++ #2 University in India 2025 #558 University Globally #1 University in India 2025 #2 University in India 2025 UGC Category-I Placement Procedure 1 The Placement Cell shall send invitations to recruiting organizations for campus internship and placement processes. Interested organizations may also send us an email at placement@jnu.ac.in 2 Organizations may register with us by filling out Job Notification Form (JNF) for internships as well as placements 3 Once the NF is filled, organizations are contacted for further processes. 4 Organizations may conduct pre-processes and for which they can request with relevant details. After selection, the company will announce the names of the selected candidates at the end of the process. course_details: Official Website of Jawaharlal Nehru University, New Delhi, India Fellowships/Scholarships Fellowships/Scholarships Student SCHOLARSHIPS FELLOWSHIPS AWARDS PROCESS FLOW FORMS NOTICES CONTACT Merit-cum-Means Scholarships: A number of Merit-cum-Means scholarships are available for deserving and meritorious students whose parent's/guardian's income does not exceed Rs. 2,50,000/- per annum or as may be laid down from time to time. The payment of scholarships will be governed by the rules in force in the University from time to time. Click for form . Ford Foundation Scholarships: A limited number of Ford Foundation Scholarships of the value of Rs. 700/- per month are available for students of the M.A. Programme of the School of International Studies. The Scholarships will be awarded purely on merit subject to the proviso that at least half of them will go to female students. In addition, the following Endowments (Scholarships)/ Fellowships/ Awards are also available to students pursuing studies in the University. Prof. Ramesha Rao Scholarship and Travel Grant: A sum of Rs.10,00,000/- was donated by Dr. (Mrs.) K. Sudha Rao, wife of Late Prof. A. Ramesha Rao of the School of Life Sciences for creation of an endowment for “A. Ramesha Rao Scholarship and Travel Grant” for students securing the highest grade in the Cancer Biology, a discipline in which Professor Rao had specialized and guided students. The award is for a period of 10 months @ Rs.3,000/- per month in a year and a travel grant of Rs.30,000/- to an M.Phil/Ph.D student for presenting paper in International Conference, Seminar, etc. in the area of Cancer Biology. This will be effective from next year 2011 with the interest accrued on the endowment. Tendulkar Scholarship : This Scholarship of Rs.400/- p.m. is awarded for a period of two years to 2 students of various Centres of School of Social Sciences by rotation. Prof. Nurul Hasan Scholarship: Two Scholarships of Rs.1500/- p.m. each are awarded for a period of one year to students of second year M.A./M.Sc./MCA students with the highest CGPA at the end of the first year, one of which shall be for Social Science subjects and the other for Science subjects, for promotion of education and other Ideals and values for which Prof. Hasan worked during his lifetime. Jawahar Bhawan Trust Scholarship: These Scholarships are of the value of Rs.1000/-p.m. awarded to 10 students for a period of one year (covering two semesters) and may be renewed for subsequent semester(s) subject to the condition that the student maintains CGPA of 5.5 at the end of third semester of MA/M.Sc. and fifth semester in case of MCA. Prof. MJK Thavaraj MCM Scholarship: There are 2 Scholarships of worth Rs.300/- each p.m. and Rs.2000/- as contingency are awarded on merit to M.A. (Economics) first year students of School of Social Sciences. D S Gardi Sanskrit Scholarship: Annually awarded to two M.A. students of Sanskrit Studies for a period of two years. These scholarships are awarded on the basis of performance of the students in the first semester. Prof. Gouri Shankar Singhal Merit Scholarship: One scholarship to M.Sc. (SLS) 2nd year student value of Rs.1000/- per month for one year who obtained highest CGPA at the end of third semester to be selected at school level on the basis firstly of merit and secondly of need. There is also a provision for students coming from weaker sections of society under the "Earn as you Learn" scheme. Sungum Sudhar Fellowship : Two scholarships for three years for those students whom PhD status is confirmed and 6.5 grade in Masters(MA & MPhil) programme. Click for Sungum Sudhar Fellowship 2019-20 SYLFF Fellowship : Ryoichi Sasakawa Young Leaders Fellowship Programme is funded by Tokyo/Nippon Foundation, Japan. Fox International Fellowship : Annually awarded to one or two students of M.Phil./Ph.D. programme in any of the following fields: economics, finance, political science, international relations, law and contemporary history. They should therefore have completed 3 semesters of M.Phil./Ph.D./Direct Ph.D. programme but should not have completed more than 6 semesters of the said programme. UGC-CSIR Junior Research Fellowship: Only those candidates will be eligible for the award of fellowships who qualify in the national level test conducted by the University Grants Commission/CSIR and other similar test for award of Junior Research Fellowship as per the norms of the relevant organization. UGC-Scholarships and Fellowship: The University Grants Commission invites applications for the award of various Scholarships and Fellowships every year for the candidates pursuing higher studies in Universities./Colleges. The details of the schemes are available at UGC website www.ugc.ac.in. State Government Fellowship: Some of the States of the Indian Union have instituted at the School of International Studies one or two fellowships for scholars coming from their respective States. Some State Governments have also provided contingency grants. course_details: About Career Services Office (CSO) Skip to content Recruitment & Internships Home > Campus > Recruitment & Internships NLSIU Campus Redevelopment 2025-2027 Reach NLSIU Campus Life Conduct & Safety Health & Wellness Mental Health and Emotional Well-Being Student Bar Association Student Clubs & Activities Sports at NLSIU Accessibility Recruitment & Internships About Career Services Office (CSO) The Career Services Office (CSO) at National Law School of India University serves as the primary interface between students and recruiters, implementing a systematic approach to recruitment and career development. The CSO facilitates internships, graduate recruitment and industry engagement across all academic programmes while strengthening long-term partnerships with employers. Our students contribute meaningfully across sectors from law firms, corporates and start-ups to NGOs, public institutions, and civil society organisations. Academic Calendar The academic year runs from July to June with three trimesters and internship breaks: July – September – Trimester I October – Break/Internship November – January – Trimester II February – Break/Internship March – May: fees=rs, rankings=mat and assessment framework. 5 | Trimester III June – Break/Internship Internships Internships are a core component of the academic programmes at NLSIU and are designed to integrate classroom learning with real-world experience in diverse professional settings. Students across the NLS BA (Hons.), BA LLB (Hons.), LLB (Hons.), and MPP programmes undertake in-person internships during the trimester breaks in February, June, and October, typically for a duration of one month. Each programme has a structured internship format and assessment framework. 5-Year BA LLB (Hons.) & 3-Year LLB (Hons.) Programmes: Students of the BA LLB (Hons.) and LLB (Hons.) programmes typically undertake compulsory internships in the month of February. These internships carry academic credits and supervisors are required to evaluate the performance of their students. All BA LLB students (across the five years) are required to undertake an internship of 20 working days in February-March every year. All LLB (Hons.) students (across the three years) are required to undertake an internship of 20 working days in February-March every year. NLS BA (Hons.) Programme: 1st Year: Immersive internship with grassroots organisations advancing social change 2nd Year: Professional internship in law, consulting, media, or policy 3rd Year: Placement internship geared toward employment after graduation Master’s Programme in Public Policy (MPP): Rural Immersion and Experiential Learning: Fieldwork internship with grassroots organisations to critically examine public policy in practice. Client-Led Projects: Students work with organisations to solve real-world policy challenges using analytical, legal and field-based skills. Graduate Recruitment NLSIU’s campus recruitment process is designed to be fair, transparent, and aligned with institutional values. Campus recruitments are conducted on a rolling basis throughout the academic year providing flexibility for recruiters and students while enabling continuous engagement with prospective employers. Our graduates build careers with leading organisations worldwide including top law firms, corporates, financial institutions, consulting firms, policy organisations, multilateral agencies and think tanks. Several graduates also pursue litigation practices, judicial clerkships, civil services and careers in academia reflecting the breadth and depth of career pathways nurtured at NLSIU. Industry Connect We host engagement events to connect students with sector experts. Formats include recruiter presentations, alumni interactions and talks on legal practice, policy careers and emerging fields. These sessions help students understand sector trends, build professional networks and make informed career choices. Partner With Us We invite organisations to partner with us for graduate recruitments, internships, industry interactions and long-term collaborations. Career Services Office: Mr. Justin Joseph Careers and Placement Officer: fees=rs, rankings=mat and assessment framework. 5 | +(91)-8329093482 Office Hours: 9:30 a.m. – 5:30 p.m., Monday through Friday Faculty In-Charge: Mr. Manish and Ms. Varsha Aithala – Law programmes Dr. Anindita Adhikari – NLS BA (Hons) programme Dr. Manpreet Singh Dhillon – MPP programme: fees=rs, rankings=mat and assessment framework. 5
